column1: # Phase 3 — macro invocation by name. A range named \<letter> is a
# macro the user runs by pressing Alt+<letter>. The macro source is
# the cell at the range's start; subsequent cells in the same column
# Stash a macro in A1: type the literal label `{GOTO}C5~hello~`. The
# leading `'` forces LABEL mode so the `~` and `{` chars stay as
# data (they only become metacharacters when the source is later
# Pressing Alt-M now runs the macro at A1. The macro: F5 (GOTO),
# type C5, Enter (commits goto), type hello, Enter (commits label).
# Case-insensitive: Alt-Shift-M is also Alt-M (only modifier matters).
# An undefined Alt-letter is a silent no-op (Lotus beeps; we don't
# fail the macro lookup, we just don't run anything).
